rows.notes: Clear grouping title followed by a monetary value on same line. The rightmost figure reads as '235.10.11' and a small loop after the pence resembles the halfpenny marker ('ob') — interpreted as 11½d. Vertical alignment with lower totals consistent.
Short entry with clearly visible '0.3.0' in the monetary columns. Handwritten name is ambiguous (read as 'Bordiners' and parenthesis uncertain — '(Ewins ?)'). Column alignment for amounts is clear.
Description letters faint but typical name 'Cook late Wyman' is plausible. Amount reads '1.6.1' aligned to the columns. Some ink spots near the description reduce certainty of the text.
Short surname visible; amount aligns to read as '0.3.2' (3s 2d). The pounds column is blank which is consistent with small shilling/pence only entries.
Surname legible and amount clearly reads '0.0.4' (4d) in the pence column. Vertical ruling/alignments are clear for this small figure.
Name read as 'Currocks' (handwriting fairly clear) and amount '0.0.6' (6d) is unambiguous in the rightmost column.
A subtotal/summa line follows the small entries. The alignment and heavier stroke on this line indicate a total. It reads '237.4.0' with a small halfpenny loop after the pence — interpreted as 0½d.
Centered section heading, no monetary amount attached. Handwriting of the header is relatively clear and separate from amounts to the right.

rows.description: of Mr Farley Vicar of Effingham the first Payment of Dr. Shatruge's Benefaction for 1795
rows.notes: Long description but legible; monetary amount clearly reads '110.0.0' aligned in the pounds column. The grouping brace on the manuscript also supports this figure.
Description and amount '10.0.0' are clear and well aligned. Handwriting for the name is legible.

rows.description: of Mr Bowles 1 year for Lt. Witt ? to Miss Last
rows.notes: Amount '350.0.0' is clearly written in the pounds column. Description is moderately ambiguous: 'Lt. Witt ?' and 'Miss Last' are my best readings from the cursive; ink is lighter here which reduces certainty.

rows.description: of Mr Demainbray Plate Money for Messrs Dawson and Bourke
rows.notes: Surname 'Demainbray' is reasonably readable; amount '20.0.0' is clear and aligned. 'Dawson and Bourke' also legible.

rows.description: of Mr Heyes & Mr Burridge's Plate Money
rows.notes: Names read as 'Heyes' & 'Burridge' though the first name has a minor ambiguity. Amount '10.0.0' is clearly written and aligned.

rows.description: of Mr Farley the 2 Payment of Dr. Shatruge's Benefaction by Mr Cooke for 1795
rows.notes: Long descriptive line but clear: second payment of the same benefaction. Amount '110.0.0' is distinct and aligned with previous similar entry.

rows.description: of Mr Demainbray Deans Fees to the House
rows.notes: Amount reads '9.6.8' (9 pounds, 6 shillings, 8 pence) — all three columns visible and within valid ranges. Description again shows 'Demainbray'; 'Deans Fees to the House' is legible.

rows.description: of Mr Cole for Mr Amyatts Plate Money
rows.notes: Description legible and amount '10.0.0' clear. Columns align with other plate-money entries.

Name read as 'Vivian Bursar' though the surname is slightly unclear. The amount reads as '176.17.8' but there is faint additional marking after the pence that could indicate a small fraction (very faint). I have recorded whole pence only and noted uncertainty.

There is a heavier or underlined figure beneath the receipts varia list which appears to be a subtotal. The exact digits are somewhat blurred and may overlap with the previous line. I have recorded the most legible reading '170.17.8' but this is moderately uncertain because of ink smudge/overlap with the previous entry.

rows.description: Mr Robt Harlins Acct for Merton Estate from Missr 94 to Missr 95
rows.notes: Section heading for estate account. Handwriting partly faded in places (particularly the dates '94' and '95') but the overall meaning is clear.
Clear two-column calculation on the left; 'Land Tax' label is legible and amount '34.7.0' is distinct and properly aligned.
Word preceding the amount is faint; read as 'Levettable' or similar. Amount to right reads '6.10.10'. Slight ink fade on description reduces confidence.
Sum of the two left-hand items; the line '40.17.10' is clearly written beneath them. Alignment consistent with a subtotal.
Right-hand column calculation: 'Money Rent' label is clear and its amount '265.0.0' is unambiguous and well aligned under the pounds column.
Label 'Corn Rent' and amount '8.13.0' are legible and properly aligned. No signs of fractional pence.
Total of rents shown as '273.13.0' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Clear bracketing on the manuscript indicates this is a subtotal for the rent side.
This deduction matches the left-hand subtotal '40.17.10'. The word 'Deduct' and the number are clearly written and bracketed in the calculation.
Result after deduction appears as '232.15.2' and is bracketed/annotated as 'Rent Clear recd' — clear and unambiguous in the right-hand calculation.

Final large total at the bottom-right of the page reads '1030.19.6' with a faint loop after the pence suggesting a halfpenny. The alignment and placement indicate a grand total, but the tail/loop on the fraction is somewhat faint so fraction recorded with moderate confidence.
